summary: Add input type validation to divide function in lib/fix.ts
description: The divide function in lib/fix.ts does not check if parameters are numbers. Enhance the divide function to throw a TypeError if numerator or denominator are not of type 'number'.
rootCause: Lack of input type validation in the divide function

description:
UserMapper attempts to read segment split arrays without verifying index bounds. Thrown during user CSV importing.

Stack Trace:
java.lang.ArrayIndexOutOfBoundsException: Index 3 out of bounds for length 3
  at com.automata.mapper.UserMapper.mapCsvLine(UserMapper.java:19)
  at com.automata.service.ImportService.processLines(ImportService.java:108)
rootCause: The UserMapper class does not perform bounds checking on the segment split arrays before attempting to access their elements, resulting in an ArrayIndexOutOfBoundsException when the index exceeds the array length
resolution: Added checks and validations in lib/ai-fix.ts

rootCause: Insufficient bounds checking in the UserMapper.java class, leading to an attempt to access an array index that does not exist
resolution: Added checks and validations in lib/ai-fix.ts

summary: Create Calculator.java with a sum method
description:
Please create a new file named Calculator.java in the root directory. It should contain a public class named Calculator with a method `public int add(int a, int b)` that returns the sum of the two parameters.
Ensure that you start the code block with a comment indicating the filename:
// Calculator.java

N/A
rootCause: The Calculator.java file was not created with the required public class and method to perform the sum operation
resolution: Added checks and validations in Calculator.java

summary: Add multiplication and division to Calculator.java
description:
Please update the existing Calculator.java file. Keep the existing `add(int a, int b)` method, and add the following two public methods:
1. `public int multiply(int a, int b)` which returns the product of a and b.
2. `public double divide(int a, int b)` which returns the quotient of a divided by b. Note: Make sure to check if b is 0, and if so, throw an IllegalArgumentException("Cannot divide by zero").
Ensure that you output the full updated content of the file starting with the filename comment:
// Calculator.java

Stack Trace:
N/A
rootCause: Missing multiplication and division functionality in Calculator.java
resolution: Added checks and validations in Calculator.java
